name: ssh_connect
description: SSH connect using a named configuration from config.yaml. Auto-detects remote OS (Linux/Windows). Supports auto-reconnect on connection loss. Use list_connections to see available config entries.

config_name.description: Name of the connection in config.yaml (e.g. 'windows-pc')

name: ssh_execute
description: Execute command on a connected SSH session. Automatically adapts to Linux (bash) or Windows (PowerShell) remote shell. Triggers auto-reconnect if connection dropped.

name: ssh_disconnect
description: Close and remove an SSH session.

name: ssh_upload
description: Upload file via SCP or SFTP. Automatically adapts path format for Linux/Windows targets.

name: ssh_download
description: Download file via SCP or SFTP. Automatically adapts path format for Linux/Windows targets.

name: ssh_list
description: List all active SSH sessions with platform and status.

name: telnet_connect
description: Connect to remote host via Telnet, persistent background session. Supports optional login. Maintains an internal buffer for accumulated data (configurable size).

name: telnet_send
description: Send data to a Telnet session. timeout=0: send and return immediately (no wait). timeout>0: send then wait for response. Supports auto-reconnect. Special values: __CTRL_C__, __CTRL_D__, __CTRL_Z__.

timeout.description: Wait timeout seconds. 0 = no wait (default), >0 = wait for response

name: telnet_listen
description: Listen on a Telnet session for a specified duration, returning all newly received data (consumer pattern: data is consumed after read). Supports multiple encodings for binary data.

encoding.description: Output encoding: utf-8 (text), base64 (binary safe), hex (binary safe)

name: telnet_disconnect
description: Close and remove a Telnet session.

name: telnet_list
description: List all active Telnet sessions with status and buffer sizes.

name: setup_com2tcp
description:
Complete com2telnet deployment workflow:
1. SSH upload com2telnet.py + pyproject.toml to D:\remote_debug\com2telnet\ on Windows PC
2. Install com2telnet + dependencies (pyserial) via pip
3. Kill any previous com2telnet instance on the same port
4. Start com2telnet in background (PowerShell Start-Process -WindowStyle Hidden)
5. Verify process is running

After setup, use telnet_connect to host:telnet_port to access serial data from the COM port.

Example: setup_com2tcp with com_port='COM4', telnet_port=5200 runs:
  com2telnet --serial COM4:5200:115200

ssh_session_id.description: SSH session ID (must already be connected to the Windows PC with the COM port)

name: list_connections
description: List all configured connections from config.yaml. Shows SSH and com2tcp entries.

name: save_config
description: Save current in-memory configuration to config.yaml.

name: telnet_start_monitor
description: Start background monitoring on a Telnet session. Data is continuously read into a circular buffer (max 900000 lines, oldest evicted when full). Optionally append to a file continuously.

output_file.description: Optional local file path to continuously append received data

name: telnet_stop_monitor
description: Stop background monitoring on a Telnet session. Returns the total line count captured.

name: list_sessions
description: List all active SSH and Telnet sessions.
